campbell.title: Campbell City Council — April 21, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to discuss and vote on changes to business license tax rates in the city. Shannon Dragman from the Finance Department presented the annual update to these tax rates, which required changes to city rules. The council deliberated publicly before making a decision on whether to approve the new rates.
keyTopics: Business license tax rates are being updated for the new fiscal year
Changes to Campbell Municipal Code needed to implement new tax rates
Finance Department presented the specific rate adjustments
Council held public discussion before voting on the changes

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to get community input on a new rule or policy. After hearing from residents, the council moved forward with the proposal by skipping the normal first-reading step and planning to vote on final approval at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback
New ordinance (city rule) introduced
First reading waived to speed up approval process
Final vote scheduled for next regular council meeting

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 21, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from earlier in the month and review the city's finances for February. The council also authorized the Town Manager to sign several contracts for construction projects and professional services.
keyTopics: Approved previous meeting minutes from April 7
Reviewed February 2026 financial and investment report
Authorized construction contracts with QLM, Inc. and other vendors
Approved a three-year professional services agreement

san-jose.title: San José City Council — May 5, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to approve routine meeting minutes and discuss several projects affecting the city. Main topics included replacing an old elevator at a terminal, finding companies to clean up homeless encampments, updating a digital advertising agreement with Clear Channel, and approving city support for a firefighter memorial event.
keyTopics: Terminal Elevator Replacement Project - infrastructure upgrade
Encampment Cleanup Services - seeking proposals for homeless camp removal
Clear Channel Digital Signage Agreement - updating large format ads on city property
Light the Night Event - memorial for fallen firefighters with city funding

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to approve meeting minutes and hear from residents during public comment. The council reviewed and made decisions on neighborhood grant requests for the 2026-27 budget year, determining which local community projects would receive city funding.
keyTopics: Neighborhood grant funding decisions for 2026-27
Public comment period for resident input
Committee reports and staff updates
Meeting minutes approval

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 28, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special evening meeting on April 28, 2026, to interview candidates for various city boards and commissions. Residents were given opportunities to comment both in person and online during the session before the meeting adjourned.
keyTopics: Board and commission candidate interviews
In-person public comment period
Online participation options for residents
Special study session format

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — May 5, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a closed-door meeting to negotiate with the Cupertino Union School District about buying or selling a property at 10480 Finch Avenue. The council also discussed the Silicon Valley Hopper program and plans to get grant funding for it.
keyTopics: Negotiating the price and payment terms for 10480 Finch Avenue property with the school district
Silicon Valley Hopper program overview and future grant funding strategy
City Manager Tina Kapoor and Interim City Attorney Floy Andrews leading property negotiations

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council met to discuss allowing large promotional signs to be displayed on buildings around Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup events. The council also approved a new contract with the firefighters union covering 2026-2029, though one agenda item was withdrawn before discussion.
keyTopics: Temporary supergraphic signs permitted near Levi's Stadium for World Cup 2026
New firefighters union contract approved for 2026-2029
One agenda item withdrawn by city staff before public hearing

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — May 5, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to approve their spending plans for the next five years and discuss the budget for the upcoming fiscal year. The council also approved a new master plan for parks and trails in the Innovation District area and made updates to how the council conducts its meetings.
keyTopics: Approved 5-year capital spending plan (2027-2031)
Reviewed next year's operating budget for FY 2026-27
Adopted Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan
Updated City Council meeting procedures and protocols

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 29, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to discuss several items including internal administrative matters like choosing new leadership. The main topic was a proposal to divide a 1-acre vacant lot on Old Trace Road into nine residential lots, which would allow a developer to build nine new single-family homes and seven small secondary dwelling units (junior ADUs).
keyTopics: Proposal to subdivide Old Trace Road lot into 9 residential parcels
Nine new single-family homes and seven junior ADUs planned for the site
Election of new City Council Chair and Vice Chair
Developer's request requires council approval before construction can proceed
